subject: Biology
title: Plants and Water Stress
description: A short lesson to help you apply Plants and Water Stress.

hook.text: Too little water wilts a plant. Too much drowns its roots. Either extreme can be fatal.

buildup.text: When water is scarce, stomata close to prevent water loss—but this also blocks CO₂ entry.

discovery.text: Desert plants have deep taproots, thick waxy coatings, and reduced leaves to conserve water.

twist.text: Resurrection plants shrivel to a crisp during drought but fully revive when water returns.

climax.text: As droughts worsen with climate change, breeding drought-resistant crops is now urgent.

punchline.text: Water decides who lives. Plants must manage every drop.

quiz.question: What happens when stomata close during drought?
options: Water is saved but CO₂ entry is blocked
